pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i wake up and for a split second i forget... and then i remember he's not coming back for coffee and everything just feels heavy. like i'm just moving through water all day. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. it feels pointless.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just stare at the ceiling and realize how stupid everything is. like, we spend our whole lives working and feeling things and stressing out, but in the grand scheme of the universe, it’s all just dust. the sun is gonna burn out eventually and everything we’ve ever done will be gone. it makes everything feel so pointless. does any of it actually matter?

pool.baseline: i feel like i’ve hit a wall and i don't think i can climb over it anymore. everything i worked for in the lab... the last three years... just gone. the funding got cut and my advisor won't even look me in the eye. i've spent my whole life being "the smart one" and now i'm just nothing. i'm just so tired of the constant fight to stay afloat.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers its last, and so do I. He is gone, and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. There is nothing left in this hollow chest but the cold mist of the moors. To exist in this darkness feels like a cruelty I can no longer endure... I find myself longing only to simply... cease. To fade away where no one can ever reach me again.
